revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:ヒエ塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Hiezuka Kofun (ヒエ塚古墳) | image = | part_of = {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11433419|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.57375|N|135.846139|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = 127m in length | built = Late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} to early {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = Pottery fragments | Historical_sites = None | notes = | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
'''Hiezuka Kofun''' (ヒエ塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara Prefecture|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Kayō-chō. It is part of the {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|1=Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, specifically the Kayō Subgroup. Unlike other tumuli in the region, it has not been Historic Site1 as a historic site.
== Overview == [[File:750314 Hieduka Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|260px|right|{{center|Aerial view of Hiezuka Kofun. {{ill|Kurazuka Kofun|ja|クラ塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is to the right, and {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is to the left.}} {{Small|{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}.}}]] The tumulus is a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, constructed on a ridge extending from Mount Ryūō at the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It is the northernmost of the larger tumuli in the Ōyamato Kofun Cluster. To the west lies {{ill|Nomugi Kofun|ja|ノムギ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomugi Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A rural road currently traverses the center of the mound, and several archaeological excavations have been conducted in the area.
The tumulus features a "spatula-shaped" front part facing west. The overall length is approximately 127 meters. Stone coverings were found on the mound's surface, but no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been identified. Surrounding the tumulus is a moat, with evidence suggesting the possible presence of an outer embankment.
The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, during the late 3rd century to the early 4th century. The Ōyamato Kofun Cluster, including Hiezuka Kofun, is associated with the tombs of early {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} rulers or influential figures closely related to the royal authority.
== Archaeological History == * '''1977 (Shōwa 52):''' Survey conducted by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' First excavation near the north side of the tumulus during road construction, conducted by the Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute. * '''2013 (Heisei 25):''' Second excavation to confirm the site’s boundaries, conducted by the Tenri City Board of Education. * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Third excavation, with findings reported in 2018. * '''2017 (Heisei 29):''' Aerial laser survey conducted by the Tenri City Board of Education. * '''2018 (Heisei 30):''' Fourth excavation and ground-penetrating radar survey conducted by the Tenri City Board of Education and {{ill|Tenri University|en|Tenri University|qid=Q4387971|lt=Tenri University|1=Tenri University}}. * '''2019 (Reiwa 1):''' Fifth excavation and additional radar survey, with results reported in 2021. * '''2020 (Reiwa 2):''' Sixth excavation, with findings reported in 2022.
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|ja|大和古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamato Kofun Cluster|qq=}} * [[Makimuku ruins]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Tenri no Kofun 100|author=Tenri City Board of Education|year=2015|chapter=Hiezuka Kofun|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref=Harvid|pages=46}} * {{Cite book|title=Kofun in the Kayō Area|author=Nara Prefecture Kashihara Archaeological Institute|year=1981|ref=Harvid}} *
*
